# UC-22.2.33 · NIS2 Art.23 — Reporting Timeline Compliance Roll-Up (24h / 72h / 1-Month)

## Description

Produces a per-incident rollup of Art.23 deadline status (24h early-warning / 72h notification / 1-month final report) for the trailing 90 days, plus aggregate compliance percentages used in board reporting. Each row classifies each deadline as ON_TIME, LATE, MISSED, or PENDING with a single-pane evidence_id.

## Value

The Head of IR and the audit committee need a single page that says 'we hit X% of our 24h deadlines, Y% of our 72h, Z% of our 1-month over the last quarter'. Without this rollup the three per-stage UCs answer the operational question but cannot answer the governance question. This UC closes that gap and produces the board-pack data.

## Implementation

(1) Schedule daily at 03:00 over a 90-day window. (2) Summary-index findings to `audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_art23_rollup` so trend queries can use `tstats` on the summary. (3) Generate a PDF dashboard digest weekly (audit committee monthly). (4) No paging — this is reporting, not operational.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• UC-22.2.1, UC-22.2.7, UC-22.2.8 implemented; their artefacts must be writing to `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_artifact` consistently.
• Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) ≥7.0; Splunk Add-on for Phantom (Splunkbase 3411) ≥3.0; Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621) ≥5.3.
• Roles: `read` on `index=notable`, `incident_review` KV-store, `index=audit_evidence`. Summary-index write requires the role to have `write` on the destination index OR the saved search runs as an `admin`-equivalent service account.
• Lookups: `nis2_significant_incident_thresholds.csv`, `nis2_exception_register.csv`, `nis2_crossborder_primary.csv`, `nis2_entity_scope.csv`.
• Network: SH must reach `index=audit_evidence` and `index=notable`.
• License: ~10 KB per finding row × ~50 incidents/quarter = trivial. Summary index storage long-term.
• Baseline knowledge: the operator's NIS2-significant-incident definition (encoded in the lookup); the operator's policy on artefact backfill and re-classification.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
• Confirm artefact-write hooks for stages `early_warning`, `72h_notification`, `final_report` are operational. Run a sanity sample: `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_artifact earliest=-90d | stats count by stage`. Should approximate 1 stage per significant incident.
• Validate cross-stage uniqueness: `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_artifact earliest=-90d | stats dc(event_id) by stage` — any large discrepancy means an artefact stage is silently failing.
• Configure the destination summary index `audit_evidence` to accept `evidence:nis2_art23_rollup` events with extended retention (typically 7 years to align with NIS2 record-keeping under Art.21(5)).
• Expected event volume: 1 row per significant incident in the trailing 90d. Steady state ~50 rows/run for a typical mid-size operator.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Use the `spl` field above. Save as scheduled search `UC-22.2.33 NIS2 Art.23 Rollup`.

Understanding this SPL — design choices:
• **90-day window** balances board-reporting cadence (quarterly) with search performance.
• **Three left-joins** (early_warning, 72h, final_report) so the rollup is a per-incident wide row with all three deadlines in one record. Auditors prefer the single-row view.
• **Four-state case() per stage** distinguishes ON_TIME / LATE / MISSED / PENDING. PENDING is in-flight (clock not expired); MISSED is past deadline without a filed artefact; LATE is filed after the deadline; ON_TIME is filed within the deadline.
• **`all_three_on_time` aggregate flag** for the single-pane KPI. `any_missed` for the alert-style KPI.
• **Backfill detection in KFP suppression** — see Step 5; the SPL itself does not handle backfills, the runbook does.

Schedule and alert action:
• Cron: `0 3 * * *` (daily 03:00). Time range `-90d to now`.
• Alert action: NONE (this is a reporting UC). Summary-index findings to `audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_art23_rollup`.
• A separate saved search `UC-22.2.33 BoardPack` runs weekly, queries the summary index, and renders a PDF via Splunk's PDF export action.

Step 3 — Validate
• Reconcile per-stage on-time counts: aggregate the rollup output and compare to UC-22.2.1/UC-22.2.7/UC-22.2.8 BREACH counts over the same window. UC-22.2.X BREACH counts should equal the rollup's `MISSED` counts per stage for the same window.
• Spot-check three incidents: pick one ON_TIME, one LATE, one MISSED. Open the artefacts in `index=audit_evidence`; manually verify the deadline math.
• Backfill detection: `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_artifact submission_was_backfilled=true | stats count by stage`. Ensure the runbook treats these correctly (see KFP 2).
• Cross-border deduplication: `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_artifact | stats values(event_id) by parent_incident_id | where mvcount(event_id) > 1` — if non-empty, ensure the rollup is filtering by primary event_id.
• Confirm summary index write: `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_art23_rollup earliest=-7d | stats count` should equal the rollup's row count over the same window.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Dashboard `compliance/nis2-art23-board.xml`:
• **Row 1 — Headline tiles:** 90-day on-time % for each stage; 90-day all-three-on-time %; total significant incidents in window.
• **Row 2 — Stacked bar:** per-week stage status (ON_TIME / LATE / MISSED / PENDING) for the trailing 13 weeks.
• **Row 3 — Heatmap:** per (urgency, nis2_tier) on-time rate. Reveals which combinations are systematically missing deadlines.
• **Row 4 — Per-incident table:** evidence_id, rule_name, urgency, nis2_tier, owner, three status columns, drilldown to ES Incident Review.
• **Row 5 — Quarterly trend:** rolling 4-week on-time % for each stage; quarterly trend lines for the audit-committee report.
• **Time picker:** Default `Last 90 days`; quarterly review uses `Last 4 quarters`.
• **Access:** `nis2_evidence_reader`, `head_of_ir`, `csirt_liaison`, `legal_grc`, `audit_committee`.

Runbook (owner: Head of IR, audit committee receives quarterly):
1. Daily SPL run summary-indexes the rollup. No alert.
2. Weekly: IR lead reviews the dashboard's `Row 4` table; investigates any LATE entries from the past week (root-cause: process, tooling, or staffing).
3. Monthly: Head of IR reviews `Row 3` heatmap with the IR team; high-MISSED cells indicate either a playbook gap (fix in UC-22.2.23) or a tier-mandate that exceeds team capacity (escalate).
4. Quarterly: PDF dashboard rendered and submitted to audit committee. Findings summary appended to the NIS2 evidence pack (`docs/evidence-packs/nis2.md`).
5. Annually: trend lines used in the operator's NIS2 self-assessment and in any peer-review or external audit.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Rollup row count drops to zero overnight.** UC-22.2.1's notable feed stopped — check `\`notable\` urgency IN ("high","critical") earliest=-24h@h | stats count`. If non-zero, the artefact joins are the problem; check `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_artifact earliest=-24h@h | stats count by stage`.
• **`MISSED` for an incident the IR team knows was reported.** Artefact-write hook is broken (check Phantom action_run logs) OR the artefact's `event_id` doesn't match the notable's. Backfill the artefact via the IR portal; the next rollup will reclassify.
• **`LATE` for an incident reported within the deadline.** Clock skew on the artefact-publishing host. Diagnose: `index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_artifact | eval lag=_indextime-_time | stats max(lag) by host`.
• **`PENDING` count stays high after the deadline expires.** The case() expression is wrong — the time-since check should be `(now() - detection_time) > 24*3600` not `> 24` (seconds vs hours). Confirm units in the SPL.
• **Rolling 4-week on-time % is flat at 100% but the team knows incidents went LATE.** The summary index isn't being updated; check `index=_audit action=search saved_search="UC-22.2.33*" | stats count by status` for skipped runs.
• **Cross-border duplicates inflate the MISSED count.** Add a deduplication step: `| dedup parent_incident_id sortby _time` after the joins.
• **Backfilled artefacts incorrectly show as LATE.** Add a `submission_was_backfilled` field check in the case() expression: `case(submission_was_backfilled=true AND isnotnull(ew_time), "ON_TIME", isnotnull(ew_time) AND ((ew_time - detection_time) / 3600) <= 24, "ON_TIME", ...)`.
• **The board pack PDF is too large to email.** Use the Splunk Cloud Files action to publish to a shared Object Storage bucket; email a link.

## SPL

```spl
`notable` urgency IN ("high","critical") earliest=-90d
| lookup nis2_significant_incident_thresholds.csv rule_name OUTPUT nis2_tier nis2_significant
| where coalesce(nis2_significant, "unclassified") != "no"
| eval detection_time = _time
| join type=left rule_name event_id [
    search index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_artifact stage=early_warning earliest=-90d
    | stats latest(_time) as ew_time by rule_name event_id
  ]
| join type=left rule_name event_id [
    search index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_artifact stage=72h_notification earliest=-90d
    | stats latest(_time) as not_time by rule_name event_id
  ]
| join type=left rule_name event_id [
    search index=audit_evidence sourcetype=evidence:nis2_artifact stage=final_report earliest=-90d
    | stats latest(_time) as fin_time by rule_name event_id
  ]
| eval ew_status = case(
    isnotnull(ew_time) AND ((ew_time - detection_time) / 3600) <= 24, "ON_TIME",
    isnotnull(ew_time), "LATE",
    (now() - detection_time) > 24*3600, "MISSED",
    1==1, "PENDING")
| eval not_status = case(
    isnotnull(not_time) AND ((not_time - detection_time) / 3600) <= 72, "ON_TIME",
    isnotnull(not_time), "LATE",
    (now() - detection_time) > 72*3600, "MISSED",
    1==1, "PENDING")
| eval fin_status = case(
    isnotnull(fin_time) AND ((fin_time - detection_time) / 86400) <= 30, "ON_TIME",
    isnotnull(fin_time), "LATE",
    (now() - detection_time) > 30*86400, "MISSED",
    1==1, "PENDING")
| eval all_three_on_time = if(ew_status="ON_TIME" AND not_status="ON_TIME" AND fin_status="ON_TIME", 1, 0)
| eval any_missed = if(ew_status="MISSED" OR not_status="MISSED" OR fin_status="MISSED", 1, 0)
| eval evidence_id = sha256(rule_name . event_id . _time . "rollup")
| table _time, rule_name, urgency, nis2_tier, owner, ew_status, not_status, fin_status, all_three_on_time, any_missed, evidence_id
| sort - _time
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Alerts.Alerts where Alerts.severity IN ("high","critical") AND _time >= relative_time(now(), "-90d") by Alerts.signature, Alerts._time
```

## Visualization

Single value: 90-day on-time % for each stage. Stacked bar: weekly stage-status counts. Heatmap: per (urgency, nis2_tier) on-time rate. Table: per-incident rollup. Quarterly trend: rolling 4-week on-time %.
